markdown:
**TL;DR** — Anthropic formalized a tiered jailbreak severity framework (CJS-0 through CJS-4) for Fable 5 — backed by a HackerOne program — while Claude Code v2.1.212 added per-session WebSearch and subagent spawn caps, signalling the platform is hardening for production multi-agent use.

## Anthropic and Claude

- **Fable 5 cyber safeguards post (July 2):** Anthropic detailed the model's "defense-in-depth" cybersecurity classifier — four tiers from always-permitted defensive ops to always-blocked ransomware/C2/wipers — and published an early draft Cyber Jailbreak Severity (CJS) scale (CJS-0 informational → CJS-4 critical, scored on capability gain, breadth, weaponisability, and discoverability); a HackerOne program is now live for submissions. [anthropic.com](https://www.anthropic.com/news/fable-safeguards-jailbreak-framework)
- **Claude Code v2.1.212 (July 17):** Major release: `/fork` copies conversations to new background sessions; `claude auto-mode reset` command; per-session WebSearch limit (default 200, tunable); per-session subagent spawn cap (default 200); MCP tool calls over 2 min auto-background; `/resume` picker for past sessions. [code.claude.com](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/changelog)
- **Claude Code v2.1.214 (July 18):** Fixed a permission-check bypass on Windows PowerShell 5.1; fixed Bash permission checks for commands >10,000 characters; added `EndConversation` tool for jailbreak-attempt handling; added periodic progress heartbeats for long-running tool calls. [code.claude.com](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/changelog)
- **Claude Code v2.1.215 (July 19):** `/verify` and `/code-review` skills no longer auto-run; must be invoked explicitly — reduces surprise skill invocations during coding sessions. [code.claude.com](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/changelog)
- **Claude for Teachers (July 14):** Anthropic launched a K-12-targeted Claude product and committed $10M to Canadian AI research in the same week. [anthropic.com](https://www.anthropic.com/news)
- **Ben Bernanke joins Long-Term Benefit Trust (July 9):** The former Fed chair added to the governance board overseeing Anthropic's mission. [anthropic.com](https://www.anthropic.com/news)

## NixOS ecosystem

- **Documentation team funding (July 10):** A renewed push to fix Nix's onboarding-documentation gap — led by @hsjobeki and @friedow — is seeking funding via Open Collective; goals include a paid maintainer, framework evaluation, and nixos-render-docs page-splitting. Thin week otherwise; no new release announcements on the blog. [nixos.org](https://nixos.org/blog/)

## AI coding agents and agent frameworks

*(Nothing on this topic from approved sources this week; items below are from non-approved sources — flag accordingly.)*

- **Coordinated model releases (July 16, non-approved source):** Multiple vendors reportedly shipped significant upgrades simultaneously — OpenAI gpt-5.5-pro, Anthropic claude-opus-4.7, Google gemini-3.1-pro-preview — with end-to-end multi-file repo agents as the headline capability; unverified from Anthropic's own news page as of this run. *(unverified — [claude-world.com](https://claude-world.com/articles/ai-coding-models-tools-july-2026/))*
- **xAI's Grok Build launched (July 8, non-approved source):** Coding agent built on Grok 4.5; positions as a direct Claude Code and Cursor competitor. *(non-approved — [aiagentstore.ai](https://aiagentstore.ai/ai-agent-news/topic/coding/2026-07-07))*
- **Google transitioning Gemini CLI → Antigravity CLI (non-approved source):** Branding and capability shift; details sparse. *(non-approved — [mightybot.ai](https://mightybot.ai/blog/coding-ai-agents-for-accelerating-engineering-workflows/))*

## Self-hosting and homelab

*(Nothing from approved sources; items below are non-approved — flagged.)*

- **Jellyfin release 2026071308 (July 13, non-approved source):** Routine point release; no changelog detail surfaced in search results. *(non-approved — [releasealert.dev](https://releasealert.dev/dockerhub/jellyfin/jellyfin))*
- **Coolify v4.0 + MCP server (May 18, ongoing):** Now supports 280+ one-click services, redesigned dashboard, native multi-server orchestration, and a new Coolify MCP Server for Claude Code integration — the MCP angle is new and worth watching for homelab-as-agent-tool setups. *(non-approved — [github.com/coollabsio/coolify](https://github.com/coollabsio/coolify))*
- No specific CVE/security advisories for popular self-hosting tools surfaced this run.

## Local and on-device LLM inference

*(Nothing from approved sources; items below are non-approved — flagged.)*

- **Ollama raises $65M (July 2026, non-approved source):** Significant funding round for the local-inference layer; validates the market for on-device serving tooling. *(non-approved — [releasebot.io](https://releasebot.io/updates/ollama))*
- **Ollama MLX backend now stable on Apple Silicon:** After shipping as a preview in early 2026, the MLX backend is promoted to stable mid-year; Gemma 4 sees ~90% faster token generation via multi-token prediction and automatic tuning on M-series chips. *(non-approved — [daily.dev](https://daily.dev/blog/running-llms-locally-ollama-llama-cpp-self-hosted-ai-developers/))*
- **Flash attention for older NVIDIA GPUs:** Ollama shipped flash attention support for pre-Ampere NVIDIA cards and iGPU vision offload — broadens the hardware floor for local vision-language models. *(non-approved — [releasebot.io](https://releasebot.io/updates/ollama))*
- **State of on-device (ongoing):** Blackwell RTX 50xx and Apple M4/M5 chips have made every local-LLM hardware guide from before March 2026 obsolete; a $600 Mac Mini now handles 14B models and a $1,600 setup runs 70B. *(non-approved — [blog.starmorph.com](https://blog.starmorph.com/blog/local-llm-inference-tools-guide))*
